title: Day 7: Inner-track + Outer-track E2E Validation
description: Execute and document both E2E tracks. Inner track: mount Fabric on werewolf-minigame-stub; configure all 6 clients; each AI client performs one demo task; verify AI actively calls fab_get_rules > 60% of task attempts (Kill Switch 1). Outer track: mount Fabric on the real /Users/wepie/Desktop/projects/werewolf-minigame (READ-ONLY file system; only ADD .fabric/ + AGENTS.md tree + client configs); verify private config files preserved; fab sync-meta updates .cursorrules and CLAUDE.md without losing private settings; fab scan on the full Cocos project completes < 10s. Document results in day7 docs.

files.change: fab init on stub; 6-client config install; per-client demo task log; fab_get_rules call rate statistics; Kill Switch 1 result
Mount Fabric on real werewolf-minigame; private config preservation verification; sync-meta timing; fab scan < 10s result; overall verdict

files.target: Stub L0 AGENTS.md (generated by fab init during inner-track test)
files.change: Committed result of running fab init on the stub; contains // TODO markers and basic L0 structure

files.target: Stub agents.meta.json (generated by fab init + sync-meta during inner-track test)
files.change: Committed result of fab init + fab sync-meta on stub; L0 node with revision hash

files.target: Stub pre-commit hook (generated by fab init during inner-track test)
files.change: Committed result of fab init; based on templates/husky/pre-commit template

implementation: Inner-track setup: run fab init on examples/werewolf-minigame-stub/; run fab config install to configure all 6 clients to point at Fabric server built from packages/server; commit generated files (AGENTS.md, .fabric/, .husky/)

Inner-track per-client demo: for each of 6 clients (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI) open the stub project; give demo task ('add a new player type component'); observe whether AI calls fab_get_rules before modifying files; record in day7-inner-track.md
Inner-track Kill Switch 1 check: count fab_get_rules calls across all 6 clients across all tasks; if <60% trigger KS-1 protocol (add hard gate fab_write_file); document result

Outer-track setup: use EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH to point at real werewolf-minigame; run fab init --target /Users/wepie/Desktop/projects/werewolf-minigame (add only); verify no existing file modified (git status on real project must show only new files added); run fab sync-meta

Outer-track verification: check that .cursorrules and CLAUDE.md were updated by sync-meta without losing existing private settings; check private config paths (.claude/.cursor/.codex) are preserved; run fab scan --target /Users/wepie/Desktop/projects/werewolf-minigame and measure wall-clock time (must be < 10s)
Document full results in day7-inner-track.md and day7-outer-track.md; include actual timing measurements, pass/fail per criterion, and Kill Switch outcomes

reference.pattern: E2E validation against real and stub fixtures; Kill Switch monitoring protocol from brainstorm §6
reference.examples: mcp-first-fabric.md §5 (7-day MVP table) and §6 (Kill Switch monitoring) for acceptance thresholds; real project at /Users/wepie/Desktop/projects/werewolf-minigame READ-ONLY

criteria: Inner-track: fab init on stub generates AGENTS.md + .fabric/agents.meta.json + .husky/pre-commit; zero existing stub files modified
Inner-track: all 6 clients successfully connect to Fabric MCP server and see 3 tools (fab_get_rules, fab_append_intent, fab_update_registry) in tools/list
Inner-track Kill Switch 1: AI actively calls fab_get_rules in >= 60% of demo task attempts across all 6 clients (30 total test tasks minimum)
Outer-track: fab init --target real-werewolf-minigame adds only new files; git status on real project shows 0 existing file modifications
Outer-track: .claude/, .cursor/, .codex/ private config files preserved with no content loss; original private settings still present after fab config install
Outer-track: fab scan on real werewolf-minigame (thousands of files) completes in < 10 seconds wall-clock time

rationale.chosen_approach: Dual-track E2E separates inner (reproducible, stub-based) from outer (real-world, READ-ONLY constraint) validation; documents Kill Switch outcomes for go/no-go decision
alternatives_considered: Single-track E2E on real project only (risky — any bug could require restoring real project files)
Automated E2E with browser automation for each client (too complex for MVP)
decision_factors: Real werewolf-minigame is READ-ONLY — inner track provides safe reproducible E2E without risk to real project
Kill Switch 1 requires actual AI client testing — cannot be unit tested; needs manual observation
< 10s scan on large Cocos project is a performance acceptance criterion that requires real data
rationale.tradeoffs: Manual AI client testing is not automatable; results depend on AI model behavior which may vary; document with screenshots/logs

manual_checks: Run fab init on stub; git status in stub dir — only new files (AGENTS.md, .fabric/, .husky/) added, no existing files modified
Open Cursor on stub project; ask 'add a new player type'; observe whether AI calls fab_get_rules — record yes/no
Repeat demo task for all 6 clients; calculate fab_get_rules call rate; must be >= 60%
Set EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH to real werewolf-minigame; run fab init; git status on real project — 0 existing files modified
Time fab scan on real werewolf-minigame: time fab scan --target /Users/wepie/Desktop/projects/werewolf-minigame — must be < 10s
success_metrics: 6/6 clients connect to Fabric MCP server and show 3 tools
>=60% fab_get_rules call rate across 30 demo tasks (Kill Switch 1 pass)
fab scan on thousands-of-files Cocos project: < 10s
0 existing file modifications in real werewolf-minigame after fab init

risks.description: Kill Switch 1 fires (AI calls fab_get_rules <60%) — requires adding fab_write_file hard gate
risks.mitigation: KS-1 protocol: add fab_write_file MCP tool that requires fab_get_rules to have been called in same session; modify client workflow per brainstorm §6 KS-1 response
risks.fallback: Strengthen MANDATORY language in tool descriptions; add self-attestation prompt in bootstrap templates

risks.description: Kill Switch 2 fires (MCP stdio latency > 2s on some client) — requires HTTP transport
risks.mitigation: Measure fab_get_rules response time during Day 2 smoke test; if > 2s implement HTTP keepalive transport
risks.fallback: Switch to HTTP transport (McpServer supports both; change transport in server/index.ts)

risks.description: fab scan performance on real werewolf-minigame (thousands of files) may exceed 10s
risks.mitigation: Apply DEFAULT_IGNORES early in file walk; use fs.opendir() streaming instead of glob for large directories; profile with --debug flag
risks.fallback: Add --max-files limit with warning; prioritize key directories
